quote.text: In every walk with nature, one receives far more than he seeks.
quote.author: John Muir

fun_fact.text: The Hungarian word 'paprika' has given us both the spice and the word used worldwide! Hungary is famous for its paprika production, with the town of Kalocsa and Szeged regions producing some of the finest varieties. The spice comes in eight different grades, from delicate and sweet to fiery hot.

short_read.title: How Harbor Seals Sleep Underwater
short_read.teaser: Harbor seals, common in the Strait of Juan de Fuca, have a remarkable ability to sleep while submerged. They can hold their breath for up to two hours and sleep vertically in the water, bobbing gently like a floating bottle!

medium_read.title: The Secret Lives of Olympic Peninsula Black Bears
medium_read.teaser: Black bears are incredibly intelligent and have an amazing sense of smell — they can detect food from miles away. This fascinating look at the bears living in Olympic National Park reveals their surprising behaviors and how they prepare for winter.

long_read.title: The Lost Art of Hungarian Embroidery
long_read.teaser: For centuries, Hungarian women created intricate embroidered patterns that told stories of their villages and regions. This beautiful exploration reveals how these traditional folk arts are being preserved and celebrated today.

port_angeles.title: Butterfly Season at Hurricane Ridge
port_angeles.description: Late June brings beautiful butterflies to Hurricane Ridge's alpine meadows. Look for colorful species like the Western Tiger Swallowtail and Painted Lady dancing among the wildflowers. The meadows are at their peak bloom right now, making it a perfect time for a scenic drive up the ridge.

puzzle_1.question: A hummingbird's heart beats approximately ___ times per minute while flying.
puzzle_1.correct_response: That's right, Barbara! Their tiny hearts beat incredibly fast to power their amazing flight abilities!
puzzle_1.close_response: You're very close! A hummingbird's heart beats about 1,200 times per minute — that's amazingly fast!
puzzle_1.wrong_response: Actually, their tiny hearts beat about 1,200 times per minute! That's 20 times every second — incredible!
puzzle_1.learn_more_text: Hummingbirds have the highest metabolism of all animals, and their hearts can beat even faster during courtship dives. Rufous hummingbirds migrate through Port Angeles every year on their journey along the Pacific Coast.

puzzle_2.question: Which Hungarian word means 'beautiful'?
puzzle_2.correct_response: Szép! That's beautiful, Barbara — and so is your knowledge of Hungarian!
puzzle_2.wrong_response: The word for beautiful is 'szép' (pronounced 'sayp'). Kedves means 'kind,' boldog means 'happy,' and erős means 'strong' — all lovely words!
puzzle_2.learn_more_text: Hungarian is a unique language in Europe, part of the Finno-Ugric family rather than Indo-European. It's known for its beautiful sounds and poetic expressions about nature and emotions.

puzzle_3.question: What type of tree is the most common in Olympic National Forest?
puzzle_3.correct_response: Wonderful! Western Hemlock thrives in the Olympic Peninsula's moist climate and can live for over 500 years in these forests!
puzzle_3.wrong_response: It's actually the Western Hemlock! These graceful trees with drooping tops are perfectly suited to our rainy climate and form the backbone of our temperate rainforests.
puzzle_3.learn_more_text: The Olympic National Forest is home to some of the most spectacular temperate rainforests in the world. Western Hemlocks can grow over 200 feet tall and provide important habitat for countless birds and wildlife.

game.title: Bubble Shooter
game.description: A relaxing bubble puzzle game — aim and pop matching colored bubbles. Easy to pick up, fun to play!
